title: Battle of Adumar
content:

The only way for Orinda to occur in 12 ABY and Nam Chorios in 13 ABY is if Nam Chorios was within a month of the division between the years, or 48:3; and Orinda must occur in 48:2, a month earlier. |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|Whatever they expect us to do, we don't do. Four, what do they expect us to do?''"
"''Run out toward the gate and get shot.''"
"''Correct. So we don't.|Wedge Antilles and Derek "Hobbie" Klivian|}} The '''Battle of Adumar''' was a conflict between the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and the [[Imperial Remnant]], fought in defense of the New Republic's ally, the newly formed [[Adumari Union]]. ==Prelude== Following the defeat of Imperial-backed [[Cartann]] in the Adumari civil war, the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Agonizer (starship)|''Agonizer'']] left [[Adumar/Legends|Adumar]] to summon an Imperial fleet to conquer the planet. This action was delayed when [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Teren Rogriss]] locked out the ''Agonizer''{{'s}} [[Holocomm/Legends|holocomm]] system with his command codes, prior to defecting to Adumar. As no one else on the ship, not even [[General/Legends|General]] [[Turr Phennir]], had the authority to override Rogriss's codes, this gave [[General/Legends|General]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] time to summon reinforcements and prepare for the coming battle. ==The Battle== When the ''Agonizer'' returned, accompanied by the Star Destroyers ''[[Retaliator]]'' and ''[[Master Stroke]]'' and numerous smaller warships, the New Republic Star Destroyer [[Allegiance (Imperial I-class)|''Allegiance'']] had been reinforced by the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] ''[[Mon Casima]]'' and its own array of cruisers and frigates. In addition, three "[[Flightknife|flightknives]]" of Adumari [[Blade-32 superiority fighter|Blade-32 fighters]] had deployed in space to join the battle, and false news reports were broadcast to give the impression that Adumar was still fighting a civil war. As a result, the Imperial forces sent a large number of their fighters, including most of their [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]], down to the surface to crush the disorganized Adumari forces and install Cartann's [[perator]] as a puppet ruler of the planet. These fighters were taken by surprise by the united Adumari forces, and suffered severe losses. In space, the New Republic's [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]], [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wings]], and shield-equipped [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] engaged the enemy [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], while the [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]] and Blade-32s carried out coordinated [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] and [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] attacks against the ''Agonizer''. The [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wings]] split their duty between attacking the ''Agonizer'' and supporting the more agile but less powerful New Republic fighters against the enemy TIEs. The New Republic's [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] focused their attention of the ''Master Stroke''. While the fighters took fairly heavy losses, especially at the hands of a flight of four [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE/D Defenders]], the Imperials lost even more fighters, and finally chose to withdraw after the ''Agonizer'' and ''Master Stroke'' lost their shields and began taking heavy damage. General Antilles shot down one TIE Defender during the battle, and severely damaged another during a one-on-one dogfight with [[General/Legends|General]] [[Turr Phennir]]. ==Aftermath== With the Imperial fleet's fighter screen in disarray and with their Star Destroyers taking heavy damage from the New Republic reinforcements and Adumari flightknives, the order to withdraw was given. The Imperial ships attempted to recover fighters then made the jump to hyperspace, leaving the Adumari Union to formally join the New Republic. ==Appearances== *''[[X-Wing: Starfighters of Adumar]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' *{{FFGTCG|set=Balance of the Force|cardname=Rogue Squadron Backup|link=balance-of-the-force/rogue-squadron-backup-balance-of-the-force-l01}} {{Po}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{GCW 13 ABY}} {{Interlang |de=Zweite Schlacht um Adumar |es=Batalla de Adumar }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Adumar, Battle of}} [[Category:Battles of Adumar]] [[Category:Battles of the Galactic Civil War]]
